subject: Accounting
title: Current vs. Non-Current Assets
description: A short lesson to help you apply Current vs. Non-Current Assets.

hook.text: Cash in the register and a ten-year-old factory are both assets—but they behave very differently.

buildup.text: Current assets can be converted to cash within a year: cash, receivables, inventory.

discovery.text: Non-current assets last longer: property, equipment, patents, and goodwill.

twist.text: A company with lots of non-current assets but little cash can't pay its bills tomorrow.

climax.text: Analysts compare current assets to current liabilities to judge short-term survival.

punchline.text: Long-term wealth means nothing if you can't pay today.

quiz.question: What defines a current asset?
options: It can be converted to cash within a year
It lasts more than five years
